questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a sales agent candidate complete in Maine?
questions.explanation: Maine requires 55 hours of pre-license education for a sales agent license. After completing the course, the candidate must pass the course final exam with at least 75% and then pass the state licensing exam within 1 year.

questions.question_text: A candidate scores 72% on her pre-license course final exam. What is the result?
questions.correct_answer: She must retake the final exam
distractors: She qualifies for the state exam
questions.explanation: A minimum score of 75% is required on the pre-license course final exam. Scoring 72% means the candidate did not meet the passing threshold and must retake the exam before she can sit for the state licensing examination.

questions.question_text: A candidate completes pre-license education but waits 14 months to take the state exam. What happens?
distractors: She can still sit for the exam
She must pay a $50 late fee
questions.explanation: Candidates must pass the state licensing exam within 1 year of completing pre-license education. After 14 months, the education credit has expired and the candidate would need to retake the pre-license course before being eligible to sit for the exam again.

questions.question_text: What is the term length for a Maine sales agent license?
questions.explanation: A Maine sales agent license is issued for a 2-year non-renewable term. Unlike associate broker and designated broker licenses, the sales agent license cannot be renewed. Agents must upgrade to associate broker or let their license expire. An extension is available for $100.

questions.question_text: A 17-year-old with a GED wants to apply for a Maine sales agent license. Is she eligible?
questions.correct_answer: No, applicants must be at least 18
distractors: Yes, a GED satisfies all requirements
No, a college degree is required
questions.explanation: Maine requires sales agent applicants to be at least 18 years old and hold a high school diploma or equivalent. Although a GED satisfies the education requirement, the age requirement of 18 is independent and must also be met.

questions.question_text: How many additional education hours are required to upgrade from sales agent to associate broker?
questions.explanation: An associate broker candidate must complete 60 hours of additional education beyond the sales agent pre-license course. The candidate must also submit a field experience form and have been an active sales agent for at least 2 of the past 5 years.

questions.question_text: A sales agent licensed for 1 year applies for an associate broker license. What is the problem with this application?
questions.correct_answer: She needs 2 years of active experience
distractors: She needs 3 years of active experience
She must wait for license expiration
questions.explanation: To qualify for an associate broker license, a candidate must have been an active sales agent for at least 2 years within the past 5 years. With only 1 year of experience, she does not yet meet the experience requirement.

questions.question_text: A designated broker decides to retire. How many designated brokers can an agency have at one time?
questions.explanation: Each real estate agency in Maine must have exactly one designated broker who is responsible for supervising all licensees within that agency. If the designated broker retires, another qualified broker must be appointed before the agency can continue operating.

questions.question_text: An associate broker with 1 year of AB experience applies for a designated broker license. She completed 50 hours of DB education. Why will MREC deny her application?
questions.correct_answer: She needs 2 years as associate broker
distractors: She needs 75 hours of DB education
She must also pass a new exam
questions.explanation: A designated broker candidate must have been an associate broker for at least 2 years within the past 5 years, plus complete 50 hours of designated broker education. With only 1 year of AB experience, she does not meet the experience threshold.

questions.question_text: Unlike sales agent licenses, associate broker licenses in Maine are:
questions.explanation: Associate broker and designated broker licenses are renewable on a biennial basis, provided continuing education requirements are met. Sales agent licenses, by contrast, are non-renewable 2-year licenses, creating an incentive to upgrade.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires a real estate license in Maine?
questions.explanation: Negotiating the sale, purchase, or exchange of real property on behalf of another person for compensation requires a license. Selling or managing your own property does not require a license because you are acting as a principal, not an agent.

questions.question_text: A friend asks you to help find tenants for her rental property and offers to pay you a finder's fee. Do you need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, rental and leasing activity is not brokerage
distractors: Yes, leasing for others for pay requires a license
Only if the rent exceeds $1,000 per month
Only if it is commercial property
questions.explanation: Maine is unusual: the Real Estate Commission has stated that transactions involving rental activity, leasing or property management are NOT real estate brokerage services requiring a real estate license. The statutory brokerage definition (32 M.R.S. Section 13001(2)) enumerates listing, promoting, procuring prospects, advertising, negotiating, options, and buying, selling or exchanging real estate — leasing is not included. Helping a landlord find tenants for a fee therefore does not require a Maine license, although it would in most other states.

questions.question_text: An unlicensed person places online ads offering to help people buy homes for a fee. Has she violated Maine law?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, advertising brokerage requires a license
distractors: No, ads alone are not violations
Only if she completes a transaction
Only if she uses the word broker
questions.explanation: Advertising brokerage services is itself a licensed activity in Maine. Even if no transaction is completed, the act of offering to perform real estate services for compensation without a license violates the law. The specific wording of the ad is irrelevant.

questions.question_text: A homeowner sells her own house without a real estate agent. Does she need a license for this transaction?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners may sell their own property
distractors: Yes, all sales require a license
Only if she advertises the sale
questions.explanation: Property owners acting on their own behalf do not need a real estate license in Maine. The license requirement applies to those who perform real estate activities on behalf of others for compensation. A for-sale-by-owner transaction is exempt.

questions.question_text: An attorney negotiates a property exchange as part of a divorce settlement and charges her normal legal fee. A broker claims she is practicing without a license. Who is correct?
questions.correct_answer: The attorney, as legal practice is exempt
distractors: The broker, all negotiation needs a license
The broker, attorneys need dual licenses
questions.explanation: Attorneys performing real estate activities incidental to their legal practice are generally exempt from licensing requirements. The divorce settlement is part of normal legal representation, not brokerage activity, so the attorney does not need a separate real estate license.
